libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Disable Ehcache's update checker in generated tests, preferably before creating any CacheManager: set System.setProperty("net.sf.ehcache.skipUpdateCheck", "true") or configure updateCheck(false). Use temporary directories for diskStore paths and always shut down CacheManager instances. Avoid Terracotta/clustered-cache tests unless a separate server setup is intentionally added.

deterministic_setup.reason: ehcache-core 2.5.0 declares JTA as provided, while its transaction APIs and XA helpers expose javax.transaction types; Java 25 does not provide these classes.

Ehcache documentation describes third-party transaction managers such as Bitronix for global transactions, and DefaultTransactionManagerLookup coverage needs an actual TransactionManager implementation outside an application server.
Bitronix declares the JMS 1.1 API as optional; adding the spec avoids class-loading gaps when Bitronix resource/transaction-manager classes are initialized during JVM or native tests.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8877 [Automation] native-image run fails for net.sf.ehcache:ehcache-core:2.5.0; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: ehcache-core also contains optional Terracotta, Hibernate, servlet, and sizeof-agent integration points; these are not required for core in-process coverage and should not be exercised accidentally without additional setup.
Bitronix and JTA 1.1 are old dependencies, so local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative for whether their metadata is sufficient.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Ehcache core is usable in-process without Docker, but meaningful coverage of its documented XA/JTA transaction path needs provided/optional transaction APIs that the artifact does not bring transitively; tests should also disable Ehcache update checks.
